content: I just got back from a month-long solo trip through Portugal and it was absolutely magical. The people, the food, the landscapes - everything exceeded my expectations.
A month in Portugal sounds incredible! What made you choose that destination?
I've been wanting to go for years but finally pulled the trigger after adopting Coco from the shelter last spring. I needed a big adventure before settling into a routine with her.
That's such a sweet reason! How's Coco adjusting to life with you?

She's amazing - a golden retriever with the most gentle soul. Though I came home to discover she'd learned how to open the lower cabinets while I was gone. My housemate wasn't too thrilled about that.
Oh no! Sounds like she missed you and was looking for snacks. Did you have any favorite spots in Portugal?
Lisbon was incredible but Sintra stole my heart. The colorful buildings, the castles on the hills, and the hiking trails were just perfect. I took so many photos there.
Sintra is breathtaking! I can only imagine your photos. Are you into photography then?
Absolutely - it's actually how I make my living as a freelance graphic designer. I incorporate my travel photography into branding projects all the time. Portugal gave me so much fresh material.
That's such a creative way to blend your passions! Do you have a favorite photo from the trip?

Probably this one of an old fisherman in a small village near Lagos. He was mending his nets and had this incredible weathered face that told so many stories. I ended up giving him a print when I left.
What a beautiful gesture! I'm sure that meant the world to him. Did you try any memorable food while you were there?

Oh my goodness, yes. The seafood was incredible - I had the best grilled sardines of my life in Setúbal. And I took a cooking class where I learned to make proper pastel de nata. My waistline might never recover!
Pastel de nata are dangerously delicious! I don't blame you at all. Did you meet many interesting people on your trip?
So many! I ended up salsa dancing almost every night in different cities. Met this amazing group in Porto who invited me to their weekly dance social. I haven't had that much fun in years.
Salsa dancing in Portugal sounds like a dream! I love how travel creates these unexpected connections. Did you have any challenges traveling solo?
Just the usual - getting lost a few times, my phone dying when I needed maps most. But honestly, the biggest challenge was deciding I had to leave. I could have stayed forever.
That's always the hardest part of a great trip! Are you already planning your next adventure?
I'm thinking Costa Rica in the spring. I want somewhere warm with good hiking and wildlife photography opportunities. Plus I need to start training for it - all that pastel de nata caught up with me!
Costa Rica would be perfect for that! The biodiversity there is incredible. Are you planning to bring Coco along or find a pet sitter?

I'll definitely need a pet sitter for that one - international travel with a dog seems complicated. But I'm already researching dog-friendly hiking spots near me so we can explore together in the meantime.
That's a great compromise! Coco will love the local adventures while you plan your bigger trips. Did Portugal inspire any new design projects?
